title: Iranian Tanker Siam Darkens AIS Evades Sanctions Near Russia
summary: Pizza in Watch tracks Iranian tanker Siam disabling AIS transponder. Vessel maneuvers near Russian waters amid sanctions evasion suspicions. Russia potentially aids Iranian oil transport operations.
contents:
## Iranian Tanker Siam Darkens AIS Evades Sanctions Near Russia

### Overview
Pizza in Watch tracks Iranian tanker Siam disabling AIS transponder. Vessel maneuvers near Russian waters amid sanctions evasion suspicions. Russia potentially aids Iranian oil transport operations.

### Event Details
Pizza in Watch reports Iranian-flagged tanker Siam abruptly darkens AIS signal while approaching Russian territorial waters. Vessel previously transited Persian Gulf, loaded suspected sanctioned oil cargo at Iranian ports. Siam maneuvers stealthily 50 nautical miles off Novorossiysk, key Russian Black Sea oil terminal. Analysts suspect coordination with Russian shadow fleet tankers for transshipment. Siam previously engaged similar evasion tactics last month near Venezuela. Russia continues facilitating Iranian oil exports despite Western sanctions through ship-to-ship transfers and flag changes. US Treasury sanctions multiple Iran-Russia tanker operators this year. Pizza in Watch posts satellite imagery confirming Siam position before blackout. Vessel speed drops to 2 knots, drifts suspiciously. Nearby Russian tanker Nord Star lingers, displays matching evasion patterns. Iran ramps oil shipments to fund proxies amid regional tensions. Shadow fleet grows with 600+ vessels, half darken AIS routinely. EU naval forces monitor Baltic routes for similar activities. Siam resumes AIS briefly, reports destination Turkey. Experts predict offload to smaller vessels for European black market. Incident highlights persistent sanctions circumvention networks linking Tehran-Moscow.

### Source Context
@pizzainwatch (Pizza in Watch) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on maritime vessel tracking ais iran russia tanker sanctions evasion ship news.
